Issue: Fix handling of `proj_attn`→`out_proj` key mapping in `load_old_state_dict` for backward compatibility in AutoEncoderKL
Decision: Map `proj_attn` → `out_proj` when both exist, initialize `out_proj` to identity/zero when only the new model has it, and silently discard `proj_attn` when only the old checkpoint has it
Rationale: Ensure backward compatibility by handling legacy attention projection keys (`proj_attn.weight`/`proj_attn.bias`) per-block in AutoEncoderKL.load_old_state_dict. If the new model has `attn.out_proj.*` and legacy `proj_attn.*` exist, copy them (initialize missing legacy bias to zeros with matching dtype/device). If the new model lacks `attn.out_proj.*` but legacy `proj_attn.*` exist, remove those legacy keys from the old state dict to avoid unexpected-key errors. The method no longer unconditionally sets `out_proj.weight` to identity/ bias to zero.
Author: ytl0623
Description: AutoEncoderKL.load_old_state_dict method in the AutoEncoderKL class
Risk: Potential risks include breaking backward compatibility if the key mapping logic is incorrect, or introducing unexpected errors if legacy keys are not properly handled (e.g., unexpected-key errors or incorrect weight initialization).
